title: Homer Electric Association announces rate increase
summary: The proposed increase, if approved by the Regulatory Commission of Alaska, will go into effect Jan. 1.

title: AST Warrant Arrest: Seward [AK25120098]
summary: On 12/21/2025, at approximately 1500 hours, Seward AST went to a residence on Camelot Rd in an attempt to contact Samuel Lawson for an outstanding bench warrant for failing to appear at a recent court hearing. Lawson was present and placed under arrest without incident, and then remanded at Spring C...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: An unexpected friendship flight: How two Chinese pilots landed in a tiny Iñupiaq village in Alaska
summary: Editor’s note: Northern Journal typically sticks to stories that won’t be reported elsewhere, since we’re a staff of two. But correspondent Max Graham and I have both spent recent weeks working on in-depth stories, and sometimes we, and readers, need something that’s a little more fun. Hence, when some strange news popped up on social media this morning, Max and I had the following text exchange: Credit: Northern Journal While Northern Journal will remain a space for sober coverage of the state’s environment and natural resources, today it’s also a space for this nice little vignette from rural Alaska. Enjoy. Max Graham Northern Jou...

title: Juneau man found dead following residential fire
summary: The cause of the fire is still under investigation.

title: Homer Elks lodge supports food pantry, local veterans
summary: Lodge #2127 recently completed disbursement of funds to the community from a $12,000 grant they received from the Alaska State Elks Association.

title: Over 60% of critical minerals demand met through global trade: IEF
summary: More than 60% of global critical mineral demands is met through international trade, underscoring the deep structural interdependence between producing and consuming economies as the deployment of clean energy accelerates, according to the International Energy Forum (IEF). This level of reliance on international trade, the group said, makes the world’s supply chains highly sensitive to geopolitical tensions, expor...

title: AST Death Investigation: Fairbanks [AK25126161]
summary: On 12/21/25 at approximately 1407 hours, the Fairbanks Alaska State Troopers responded to a report of a deceased person in the Fairbanks area. Investigation determined that the death was natural and foul play was not suspected. The State Medical examiner was notified, an autopsy declined and the rem...
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: In Alaska’s warming Arctic, photos show an Indigenous elder passing down hunting traditions
summary: Annika Hammerschlag Associated Press Originally published on the Associated Press. KOTZEBUE, Alaska (AP) — The low autumn light turned the tundra gold as James Schaeffer, 7, and his cousin Charles Gallahorn, 10, raced down a dirt path by the cemetery on the edge of town. Permafrost thaw had buckled the ground, tilting wooden cross grave markers sideways. The boys took turns smashing slabs of ice that had formed in puddles across the warped road. Their great-g...

title: How 2026 will reshape US critical minerals resilience
summary: Heading into year two of the current US administration, the federal government is poised to sharpen its focus on critical minerals as policy, investment, and security priorities converge. Policy momentum, new public-private funding tools, and growing concerns over critical mineral dependencies are prompting Washington to think beyond rare earths and toward bro...

title: AST Residential Structure Fire: Hooper Bay [AK25125524]
summary: On Friday, December 19, 2025, at approximately 0230 hours, a residential structure fire was reported in the community of Hooper Bay. The Alaska State Troopers and a Deputy Fire Marshal from Fairbanks responded to conduct an origin and cause investigation. The structure was unoccupied at the time of....

incident_type: Assault 4th (DV) and Assault in the Presence of a Child (x2)
summary: On 12/20/2025 at 2308 hours the Alaska State Troopers responded to a disturbance in Cantwell. Investigation determined Robert Frazer, age 35 from Cantwell caused physical injury to a family member in the presence of two children. Frazer was arrested and remanded to FCC where he was held without bail...

title: AST Assault 2 (DV): Wasilla [AK25126053]
summary: On December 21st, 2025 at approximately 0100 hours, AST responded to a report of a rolling disturbance in the Wasilla area. AST contacted 23 year old Conner Creek, of Willow, in his vehicle. Investigation revealed that Conner had assaulted a household member. He was arrested and remanded to Mat-Su Pretrial on one count of Assault in the Second Degree. He has no bail.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: Update: CBJ cancels air quality emergency in Mendenhall Valley Sunday morning
summary: The poor air quality was caused by an air inversion, trapping pollutants at lower elevations.

title: City council passes 2026 city and utility budgets
summary: Sign leading to Ketchikan City Hall shown on Dec. 9 2025. (Hunter Morrison/KRBD) The Ketchikan City Council approved the city’s general government and utilities budgets for 2026 during its Thursday night meeting. The city oversees several public services, like utilities and street maintenance. One o...

title: AST Failure to stop: Wasilla [AK25126019]
summary: On 12/20/2025 at approximately 2058 hours, the Alaska State Troopers conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle that was driving recklessly through traffic in downtown Wasilla. The vehicle failed to stop and fled from Troopers, continuing to drive recklessly and in the opposite lane of travel on winding,...
